scenarios.name: Airport & Arrival
scenarios.description: Get through arrival and into the city.
scenarios.tip: Keep arrival questions short: luggage, ATM, SIM, pickup.

scenarios.name: Transport
scenarios.description: Handle rides, buses, and stations with less confusion.
scenarios.tip: Show the map, speak in short chunks, and confirm the stop before the ride moves on.

scenarios.name: Prices & Payment
scenarios.description: Check prices and pay without getting lost in the numbers.
scenarios.tip: Point to the amount when you can.

scenarios.name: Food & Drink
scenarios.description: Order, adjust the meal, and fix the order when needed.
scenarios.tip: Start with the main order, then add one small change if you need it.

scenarios.name: Health & Pharmacy
scenarios.description: Cover common travel symptoms and pharmacy needs.
scenarios.tip: Keep symptom phrases short and direct.

scenarios.name: Emergency & Safety
scenarios.description: Keep emergency phrases short and easy to reach.
scenarios.tip: Say what is wrong right now, not the whole story.

scenarios.name: Phone & Internet
scenarios.description: Get your phone, data, or battery working again.
scenarios.tip: The fastest fixes are usually Wi-Fi, SIM, charger, or outlet.

scenarios.name: Repeat & Clarify
scenarios.description: Repeat, slow down, or move the conversation to writing.
scenarios.tip: When one word or number is the problem, ask for it again or ask to see it written.

scenarios.name: Get Help
scenarios.description: Get practical help when something goes wrong.
scenarios.tip: Name the problem first, then ask for the help you need.

scenarios.name: Hotel & Room
scenarios.description: Handle check-in, room issues, and front desk fixes.
scenarios.tip: Start with the room or reservation, then ask for the next step.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.description: Get where you need to go one turn at a time.
scenarios.tip: Ask one step at a time instead of the whole route.

scenarios.name: Bathroom & Basics
scenarios.description: Handle the small needs that still matter on the road.
scenarios.tip: Keep these requests short because the moment is usually immediate.

scenarios.name: Time & Bookings
scenarios.description: Confirm times, dates, and booking details.
scenarios.tip: Pair the time with a date, room, bus, or booking.

scenarios.name: Shopping
scenarios.description: Compare size, color, fit, and checkout.
scenarios.tip: Use these to choose, try on, and pay.

scenarios.name: Everyday Errands
scenarios.description: Handle the little errands that keep the trip moving.
scenarios.tip: Keep errands short: item first, action second.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.description: The short polite phrases you use all day.
scenarios.tip: Short polite phrases work better than long textbook sentences.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Short friendly comments work better than deep small talk.

scenarios.name: Sightseeing
scenarios.description: Use simple phrases for tickets, times, and meeting points.
scenarios.tip: Ask about the price, the time, and where to start before you begin.

notes: First real Viet v2 pack source. Scenario remains the runtime spine while the visible app language can bias toward travel situations and high-stress retrieval.
